summary: Iter17: Full UI-driven E2E delete verification requested by user is GREEN. Drove real Playwright browser through the entire flow at the public preview URL. STEP1 super admin sign-in via UI landing → /admin-pin-login → tabs OK. STEP2 seeded 3 test companies + 2 employees via backend API. STEP3 (Employee delete): Navigated to /admin, tapped testID=`delete-emp-<uid>`, single window.confirm 'Delete DelTest Employee? This will also remove their attendance, leaves, tickets and payslips. Cannot be undone.' → row disappeared, backend GET /admin/employees confirms user is gone. STEP4 (Empty company delete): /companies → tapped testID=`delete-company-<cid>` on 'DelTest Empty Co', ONE confirm 'Delete "DelTest Empty Co"?' → card disappeared, backend confirms gone. STEP5 (Force delete): tapped delete on 'DelTest ForceCo' (1 linked employee) → FIRST confirm 'Delete "DelTest ForceCo"?', SECOND confirm '1 employee(s) still linked to this company. Confirm again to cascade-delete them along with the company. Proceed with FORCE DELETE?' → card disappeared, backend confirms BOTH company and employee gone. STEP6 regression: DELETE unknown company_id → 404, unauth DELETE → 401, UI sign-out from /pin-change screen returns cleanly to landing with 'Admin sign in' button visible (iter_15 regression clean). BACKEND: baseline pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_delete_flows_iter16.py 9/9 PASS. FINAL CLEANUP: all DelTest* companies + employees + phone/email test users purged from Mongo; super admin doc reset to {pin_must_change:true, pin_fail_count:0, pin_locked_until:null}; final POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login → 200 with pin_must_change=true. One PRE-EXISTING backend bug surfaced during seeding (NOT related to delete flow): users collection has a non-sparse unique index `email_1` that raises DuplicateKeyError on the SECOND phone-only employee-signup (both docs have email=None). Reported below.

minor.issue: MongoDB users collection has unique index `email_1` WITHOUT sparse:true / partialFilterExpression. Result: the SECOND (and every subsequent) employee-signup where `email` is omitted/null causes a 500 Internal Server Error (pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError: E11000 dup key { email: null }). Reproduction: two consecutive POSTs with distinct phones and no email → first succeeds, second → 500. Fix: convert to sparse unique index OR partialFilterExpression on {email:{$type:'string'}}. See /app/backend/server.py:1176-1211 and `db.users.getIndexes()` output. Pre-existing, unrelated to the current delete regression, but real-world signups where employees skip email will collide.

react_native_issues.issue: Pre-existing deprecation warning carried over from iter_16 — non-blocking.
Pre-existing deprecation warning carried over from iter_16 — non-blocking.

action_items: OPTIONAL BUT RECOMMENDED (real signup bug): make the users collection `email` unique index sparse (or add partialFilterExpression on email type=string). Currently, two employees signing up by phone-only both write email:null → second signup returns 500. Fix location: wherever indexes are declared (search for `create_index` / `ensure_index` on users) OR run a one-time migration: `db.users.dropIndex('email_1'); db.users.createIndex({email:1}, {unique:true, sparse:true})`.
OPTIONAL (carried from iter_16): server.py is 2366 lines; splitting into auth/companies/attendance modules is still recommended.
critical_code_review_comments: The two-step force-delete confirm on /companies (companies.tsx:196) relies on substring match `msg.includes('still linked')` against the backend 409 detail. This works today but couples FE and BE by a string. Consider returning a structured error code (e.g. `{error_code:'employees_linked', linked_count:N}`) so the FE can key on a stable field. Not blocking — flagged also in iter_16.
employee-signup accepts `email` as optional but the Mongo unique index does not treat null as sparse; that mismatch is the root cause of the DuplicateKeyError described in backend_issues.minor.
updated_files: /tmp/seed_data.py, /tmp/seed_data.json, /tmp/verify_and_cleanup.py (ephemeral scripts; kept for next agent reuse)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 UI steps: sign-in, emp delete, empty co delete, force co delete, sign-out)

seed_data_creation: Created and DELETED during this run: (a) 'DelTest Ltd' + employee '+919999223311/DelTest Employee' (deleted employee via UI, deleted company via cleanup script), (b) 'DelTest Empty Co' (deleted via UI single-confirm), (c) 'DelTest ForceCo' + employee '+919999223399/Force Emp' (deleted via UI two-confirm force cascade). Final DB state verified: GET /api/companies → 0 items, no test phones/emails remaining, super admin restored to {pin_must_change:true, pin_fail_count:0, pin_locked_until:null}, final admin-pin-login → 200.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Delete flows (employee, empty company, force cascade company) are fully verified end-to-end via the actual UI at the public preview URL, not just backend. testIDs used: `delete-emp-<user_id>` on /admin, `delete-company-<company_id>` on /companies. Force-delete triggers a SECOND window.confirm on web whose message includes 'FORCE DELETE'. Landing screen sign-out is available even from /pin-change via testID=`pin-change-signout` (useful when a fresh super admin with pin_must_change=true wants to bail without burning the temp PIN). Only newly-discovered issue is the non-sparse email_1 unique index — pre-existing, orthogonal to delete work, but WILL break multi-employee signups by phone in production if not fixed. Super admin creds unchanged: sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / 246810 / pin_must_change=true.
rca_of_issue: No delete-flow bugs found. UI works end-to-end as designed. The only anomaly encountered was in the SEED path (not the SUT): two consecutive phone-only employee-signups collided on `email:null` because the Mongo unique index on users.email is not sparse. Worked around by giving each seed employee a distinct `email` value. This is a legitimate production defect but NOT related to the delete regression the user asked to verify.
